description: Curated ECB series for Euro Area macro analysis. Priority 1 = essential for any EA macro note. Priority 2 = useful for deeper analysis. Priority 3 = supplementary.

series.description_en: Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices, all items, Euro Area. Year-on-year rate of change. The ECB's primary inflation target variable. Use this for any analysis of EA inflation dynamics, monetary policy decisions, or purchasing power.
series.notes: ECB inflation target: 2% symmetric (since 2021 strategy review). Published ~3 weeks after reference month. Flash estimate available ~2 weeks after month end.

series.name_es: Inflación IAPC subyacente, Área Euro (% interanual)
series.description_en: HICP excluding energy and food. Euro Area. Year-on-year rate. Measures underlying inflationary pressure, stripping out volatile components. Key indicator the ECB watches to assess persistence of inflation.
series.notes: Often referred to as 'super-core' when services are further excluded. Tends to be stickier than headline — important for assessing disinflation durability.

series.description_en: HICP energy component, Euro Area, year-on-year change. Highly volatile, strongly correlated with oil and gas prices. Key driver of headline inflation swings.
series.notes: Drove the 2021-2022 inflation surge. Large base effects make YoY comparisons misleading — always check MoM alongside.

series.description_en: HICP food including alcohol and tobacco, Euro Area, year-on-year change. Important component for lower-income household inflation assessment.
series.notes: Includes alcohol and tobacco. For unprocessed vs processed food split, separate series are available.

series.name_es: Tipo de facilidad de depósito del BCE (%)
series.description_en: ECB Deposit Facility Rate, level. The rate banks receive for depositing overnight funds with the ECB. Since 2022 hiking cycle, this is the ECB's main policy rate signal. Essential for any monetary policy analysis.
series.notes: Primary ECB policy rate since the 2022-2023 hiking cycle. Moved from -0.5% (Jun 2022) to 4.0% (Sep 2023), then cutting cycle began Jun 2024.

series.name_es: Tipo de operaciones principales de financiación del BCE (%)
series.description_en: ECB Main Refinancing Operations (MRO) rate. The rate banks pay to borrow from the ECB for one week. Historically the key policy rate before the DFR took prominence.
series.notes: Together with DFR and MLF forms the ECB interest rate corridor.

series.name_es: Tipo de facilidad marginal de crédito del BCE (%)
series.description_en: ECB Marginal Lending Facility rate. The rate banks pay for overnight borrowing from the ECB. Forms the ceiling of the ECB interest rate corridor.
series.notes: Upper bound of the ECB rate corridor. Together with DFR forms the corridor within which overnight rates trade.

series.description_en: Euro Interbank Offered Rate, 3-month tenor, monthly average. Benchmark rate for mortgages, consumer loans and corporate credit across the Euro Area. Key transmission channel of ECB monetary policy.
series.notes: Reference rate for ~70% of Euro Area mortgages. Highly relevant for household financial conditions. Moved from negative territory (-0.57% Jan 2022) to above 4% in 2023.

series.description_en: Euro Interbank Offered Rate, 12-month tenor, monthly average. Used as reference for longer-term loans and mortgages. Reflects market expectations for ECB rates over the next year.
series.notes: Contains forward-looking information about expected ECB rate path. Spread vs 3M Euribor signals market rate expectations.

series.name_es: Oferta monetaria M3, Área Euro (% interanual)
series.description_en: Broad money supply M3 for the Euro Area, year-on-year growth. Includes currency, deposits, money market funds and short-term debt securities. Leading indicator of future inflation according to monetarist theory; ECB's reference aggregate.
series.notes: ECB's reference value for M3 growth was 4.5% (discontinued as pillar but still monitored). Contraction in M3 preceded past recessions. Key for assessing monetary conditions.

series.name_es: Oferta monetaria M1, Área Euro (% interanual)
series.description_en: Narrow money M1 (currency in circulation + overnight deposits), Euro Area, year-on-year growth. More volatile than M3 but faster leading indicator of economic activity.
series.notes: Sharp M1 contraction in 2022-2023 was an early recession warning signal. More sensitive to rate changes than M3.

series.name_es: Crecimiento del PIB, Área Euro (% interanual)
series.description_en: Gross Domestic Product, Euro Area, year-on-year growth rate. Chain-linked volumes, seasonally and working-day adjusted. The broadest measure of economic activity in the Euro Area.
series.notes: Flash estimate released ~30 days after quarter end. Full release with expenditure breakdown ~60-70 days after. Subject to revisions.

series.name_es: Crecimiento del PIB, Área Euro (% trimestral)
series.description_en: Gross Domestic Product, Euro Area, quarter-on-quarter growth rate. Chain-linked volumes, seasonally and working-day adjusted. Two consecutive negative quarters define a technical recession.
series.notes: Two consecutive negative QoQ readings = technical recession. More timely than YoY for detecting turning points.

series.description_en: Harmonised unemployment rate, Euro Area, total (ages 15-74). Seasonally adjusted. Measures share of labour force that is unemployed and actively seeking work. Key labour market indicator for ECB dual mandate assessment.
series.notes: Eurostat harmonised definition. Published ~30 days after reference month. Euro Area unemployment hit historic low ~6.4% in 2023.

series.name_en: Loans to Non-Financial Corporations, Euro Area (YoY %)
series.name_es: Crédito a sociedades no financieras, Área Euro (% interanual)
series.description_en: Bank loans to non-financial corporations (NFC), Euro Area, year-on-year growth. Key indicator of credit conditions and business investment financing. Transmission channel of monetary policy to the real economy.
series.notes: Rapid deceleration in 2022-2023 confirmed monetary tightening was transmitting to the real economy. ECB Bank Lending Survey complements this with qualitative data.

series.name_es: Crédito a hogares, Área Euro (% interanual)
series.description_en: Bank loans to households, Euro Area, year-on-year growth. Includes mortgages and consumer credit. Key indicator of household financial conditions and housing market health.
series.notes: Dominated by mortgage lending. Sensitive to Euribor movements given prevalence of variable-rate mortgages in EA.
